paper: Neural Population Dynamics during Reaching Are Better Explained by a Dynamical System than Representational Tuning
result_section: Disrupting the underlying condition structure - covariance-matched permutation test
claim: Within the paper's simulation framework, CMPT separates representational rotations that reappear after covariance-matched permutation from dynamical rotations that do not.
evidence_summary: The figure directly shows that representational and dynamical simulations can both look rotational before control, but only the representational case regains rotational structure under covariance-matched permutation.
variable_or_neural_object: simulated representational model; dynamical model; condition structure; RGR
analysis_method: CMPT; covariance-matched permutation; RGR / rotational-structure comparison
relevance_to_research_question: Establishes the key statistical-control logic needed to interpret rotation claims in population-dynamics analyses.
uncertainty: This is a simulation/control result, not direct biological evidence.
notes: CMPT-based methodological evidence distinguishing two simulated explanatory frameworks.

paper: Neural Population Dynamics during Reaching Are Better Explained by a Dynamical System than Representational Tuning
result_section: Number of neurons and conditions required for statistically significant rotations

notes: Statistical-power / sample-size caution; avoid overcommitting to a single hard threshold.
